reason: The write-enabled Codex pass (gpt-5.5, xhigh) ran for the full duration and applied real in-file edits (notably a corrected hinge sign -beta*max(0,Gamma-H) -> +beta*max(0,Gamma-H) with a derivative-based justification, verified numerically), but the outer `timeout 590` wrapper sent SIGTERM (exit 143) to the parent shell at the deadline before Codex flushed its completion changelog, so a clean completed pass cannot be confirmed. Fell back to a careful manual re-derivation: entropy gradient dH/dz_i=p_i(-log p_i-H), reverse-KL D_KL(p||u)=-H(p)+log K, forward-KL/label-smoothing H(u,p)=D_KL(u||p)+H(u), plain penalty -beta*H, and the corrected hinge sign all re-verified by hand and (for the hinge) numerically. Re-gate when the runtime can run to completion.
